questions.questionText: A square has a side length of 7 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.explanation: The perimeter of a square is 4 times its side length. Perimeter = 4 × 7 cm = 28 cm.

questions.questionText: A rectangle has a length of 10 cm and a width of 4 cm. Calculate its area.
questions.explanation: The area of a rectangle is length × width. Area = 10 cm × 4 cm = 40 $\text{cm}^2$.

questions.questionText: What is the area of a triangle with a base of 6 cm and a perpendicular height of 5 cm?
questions.explanation: The area of a triangle is ½ × base × height. Area = ½ × 6 cm × 5 cm = 15 $\text{cm}^2$.

questions.questionText: A circle has a radius of 3 cm. Using $\pi$ = 3.14, what is its circumference?
questions.explanation: The circumference of a circle is $2 \pi r.$ Circumference = 2 × 3.14 × 3 cm = 18.84 cm.

questions.questionText: A rectangle has a perimeter of 24 cm. If its length is 8 cm, what is its width?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 2(length + width). 24 = 2(8 + width). 12 = 8 + width. Width = 4 cm.

questions.questionText: Calculate the area of a circle with a diameter of 10 cm. Use $\pi$ = 3.14.
questions.explanation: Radius = diameter / 2 = 10 cm / 2 = 5 cm. Area = $\pir^2$. Area = 3.14 × $(5 cm)^2$ = 3.14 × 25 $\text{cm}^2$ = 78.5 $\text{cm}^2$.

questions.questionText: A path around a garden is 2 m wide. If the garden is a square with side length 10 m, what is the perimeter of the garden including the path?
questions.explanation: The side length of the garden including the path will be 10 + 2 + 2 = 14 m. Perimeter = 4 × 14 m = 56 m.

questions.questionText: A semi-circle has a radius of 7 cm. What is its area? Use $\pi$ = $\frac{22}{7}$.
questions.explanation: Area of full circle = $\pir^2$ = ($\frac{22}{7}$) × $7^2$ = 22 × 7 = 154 $\text{cm}^2$. Area of semi-circle = ½ × 154 $\text{cm}^2 = 77 \text{cm}^2$.

questions.questionText: A parallelogram has a base of 8 cm and a perpendicular height of 3 cm. What is its area?
questions.explanation: The area of a parallelogram is base × height. Area = 8 cm × 3 cm = 24 $\text{cm}^2$.

questions.questionText: The area of a square is 36 $\text{cm}^2$. What is the length of one side?
questions.explanation: The area of a square is side × side. So, side = $\sqrt{A}$rea. Side = $\sqrt{36} \text{cm}^2$ = 6 cm.

questions.questionText: A trapezium has parallel sides of length 5 cm and 9 cm, and a perpendicular height of 4 cm. Calculate its area.
questions.explanation: The area of a trapezium is ½ × (sum of parallel sides) × height. Area = ½ × (5 + 9) cm × 4 cm = ½ × 14 cm × 4 cm = 28 $\text{cm}^2$.

questions.questionText: A compound shape is made of a rectangle (length 6 cm, width 4 cm) and a square (side 3 cm) attached to one of its sides. What is the total area of the compound shape?
questions.explanation: Area of rectangle = 6 cm × 4 cm = 24 $\text{cm}^2$. Area of square = 3 cm × 3 cm = 9 $\text{cm}^2$. Total area = 24 $\text{cm}^2 + 9 \text{cm}^2 = 33 \text{cm}^2$.
